name: SYRIAN AMERICAN MEDICAL SOCIETY FOUNDATION
mission: SAMS works to serve the medical needs of patients in need, regardless of religious background, race, ethnicity, or political affiliation.
theoryOfChange: SAMS states it saves lives and alleviates suffering by delivering dignified, essential healthcare during crises, revitalizing health systems, and promoting medical education through a network of humanitarians; it also describes empowering local healthcare professionals through training and capacity-building to foster resilient health systems.

scoreSummary: SYRIAN AMERICAN MEDICAL SOCIETY FOUNDATION shows exceptional alignment and strong impact, with zakat compliance (minor risk deductions applied).

evaluationSources: Hakam Sibai Award for Service (Syrian American Council (SAC))

zakatClaimEvidence: Dedicated zakat page found at https://www.sams-usa.net/zakat (Source: https://www.sams-usa.net/zakat)

ui_signals_v1.recommendation_rationale: Good donor alignment with manageable risk. Evidence quality is solid but still evolving in places.

keyConcerns.headline: 73% of expenses stay in the US despite international operations
keyConcerns.detail: Organization claims foreign operations but most spending remains domestic.

components.evidence: Explicit zakat program (+4); Muslim-focused organization (+2); Asnaf: fuqara (+5); Operates in Muslim-majority regions (+3); Strong humanitarian service (+4)
components.name: Muslim Donor Fit

components.improvement_suggestion: Expand services to underserved populations or geographies with limited nonprofit coverage.
components.name: Underserved Space

alignment.rationale: Alignment 43/50: HIGH Muslim donor fit, global health cause area

components.evidence: No beneficiary data available for cost-per-beneficiary scoring; high program ratio (≥85%) suggests efficient delivery
components.improvement_suggestion: Publish beneficiary counts consistently so cost-effectiveness can be assessed.
components.name: Cost Per Beneficiary

components.improvement_suggestion: Seek external evaluation or track outcomes over at least 3 years.
components.name: Evidence & Outcomes

judge_issues.message: The rationale does not mention the risk deduction of -5 due to the high domestic burn rate (73% of expenses staying in the US despite international operations). This is a key factor affecting the overall score and should be acknowledged.

risks.risk_summary: Key concern: High domestic burn: 73% of expenses stay in the US despite international operations

risks.description: High domestic burn: 73% of expenses stay in the US despite international operations

score_details.score_summary: SYRIAN AMERICAN MEDICAL SOCIETY FOUNDATION shows exceptional alignment and strong impact, with zakat compliance (minor risk deductions applied).

zakat.claim_evidence: Dedicated zakat page found at https://www.sams-usa.net/zakat (Source: https://www.sams-usa.net/zakat)

all_citations.claim: SAMS provides primary and specialty healthcare and maintains strong financial metadata.
The charity is Zakat-eligible and maintains a specific Zakat policy.
The organization reported total revenue of $61,395,982 and a program expense ratio of 95.6%.
SAMS provides medical services inside Syria and operates specialized mental health programs.
all_citations.source_name: Human Devastation Syndrome: The Impact of Conflict on Mental Health

all_citations.claim: SAMS supports children's groups in Jordan and Syria as part of its humanitarian mission.
Therapy programs led by SAMS have resulted in a reduction in PTSD symptoms among participants.
The organization offers various donation methods and options for supporters.

baseline_narrative.amal_score_rationale: SAMS earns a high overall score due to its direct service model, exceptional financial efficiency, and strong alignment with urgent global health needs. The organization demonstrates high transparency and a proven track record in conflict-affected regions.
areas_for_improvement: Expanding public reporting on long-term cost-per-beneficiary metrics for specialized treatments

dimension_explanations.alignment: The organization is highly aligned with the needs of Muslim donors, as it is Zakat-eligible and adheres to a clear Zakat policy [2][8]. Its focus on global health in high-urgency zones and its commitment to serving all patients regardless of religious or political affiliation demonstrates a strong humanitarian track record [4][6].
dimension_explanations.impact: SAMS provides direct medical services that have shown measurable success, including a documented reduction in PTSD symptoms among therapy recipients [7]. The foundation operates with high financial health, maintaining 4.6 months of working capital and ensuring that the vast majority of its $61,395,982 revenue goes directly to programs [1][3].

baseline_narrative.headline: The Syrian American Medical Society Foundation provides life-saving medical relief and specialized healthcare to vulnerable populations regardless of their background.
strengths: High program efficiency with 95.6% of expenses directed toward medical services
Exceptional fundraising efficiency of $0.02 per $1 raised
Strong focus on Mental Health and Psychosocial Support (MHPSS) with documented clinical outcomes
baseline_narrative.summary: The Syrian American Medical Society (SAMS) Foundation is a humanitarian organization dedicated to delivering primary and specialty healthcare, medical missions, and mental health support to patients in need [1][4]. In the most recent fiscal year, the foundation reported a total revenue of $61,395,982 while maintaining a high program expense ratio of 95.6% [3].

rich_narrative.amal_score_rationale: SAMS earns a high overall score due to its direct service model, exceptional financial efficiency, and strong alignment with urgent global health needs. The organization demonstrates high transparency and a proven track record in conflict-affected regions.

areas_for_improvement.detail: While program ratios are strong, the charity should <cite id="15">publish consistent beneficiary counts</cite> to allow for standardized cost-per-beneficiary assessments.
To reach the highest evidence tier, SAMS could <cite id="16">seek external evaluations</cite> of its long-term medical outcomes beyond internal tracking.

bbb_assessment.summary: Maintains strong governance with 100% independent board and audited financial statements.

case_against.mitigation_notes: This is partially explained by the foundation's role in medical missions and procurement of US-based medical supplies for shipment.
risk_factors: <cite id="23">95.6% of expenses stay in the US</cite> according to risk assessments
case_against.summary: The primary concern is a <cite id="23">high domestic burn rate</cite>, with a significant portion of expenses remaining in the US despite the international mission.

confidence.data_freshness: Financial data from 2023; Impact data from 2018 reports
confidence.notes: Strong financial and governance data; impact data is detailed but needs updating.

alignment.explanation: SAMS is a premier fit for Muslim donors, offering a <cite id="20">verified zakat program</cite> that serves the <cite id="21">fuqara (poor) and masakin (needy)</cite> in Muslim-majority crisis zones.
alignment.improvement: The organization could <cite id="22">expand services to additional underserved geographies</cite> with limited nonprofit coverage to maximize its alignment score.

credibility.explanation: Donors can trust SAMS due to its <cite id="8">Platinum Seal of Transparency</cite> and long <cite id="1">28-year track record</cite> of medical service. Their financial statements are <cite id="18">independently audited</cite> and they maintain high governance standards.
credibility.improvement: Credibility could be further enhanced by publishing more recent multi-year outcome reports to supplement their 2018 data.

impact.explanation: The organization is highly efficient, with a <cite id="4">95.6% program expense ratio</cite> and a <cite id="12">resilient working capital ratio of 4.6 months</cite>. They focus on <cite id="19">direct service delivery</cite> in high-need areas.
impact.improvement: Impact measurement would benefit from <cite id="15">tracking cost-per-beneficiary</cite> across different medical specialties.

donor_fit_matrix.evidence_rigor: Grade C - Internal outcome tracking and MHPSS clinical data

grantmaking_profile.grant_strategy: Distributes significant foreign grants to local medical implementing partners

rich_narrative.headline: The Syrian American Medical Society Foundation provides life-saving medical relief and specialized healthcare to vulnerable populations regardless of their background.

ideal_donor_profile.best_for_summary: Best for donors who want to support high-specialty medical interventions and mental health support in conflict zones through a zakat-eligible, professionally-run medical society.
donor_motivations: Want to provide specialized healthcare (like dialysis or MHPSS) to refugees
Value organizations with a strong presence inside Syria and Gaza
Prefer to give to a charity with a Platinum transparency rating
Seek to fulfill zakat through medical aid for the needy
giving_considerations: The organization has seen rapid growth, which may affect long-term overhead ratios
Focus is on acute crisis zones with high operational risks
Administrative costs are low, but domestic spending is relatively high for an international org
ideal_donor_profile.not_ideal_for: Donors seeking small-scale, local US community health clinics or those who require third-party RCT evidence for medical interventions.

impact_evidence.evidence_grade_explanation: Grade C reflects that while SAMS tracks specific outcomes like PTSD reduction and service counts, it lacks independent third-party impact evaluations.
impact_evidence.theory_of_change_summary: SAMS utilizes a network of medical professionals to provide primary, secondary, and specialized care while training local staff to ensure sustainable health infrastructure in crisis zones.
impact_evidence.why_evidence_matters: Evidence ensures that medical interventions actually result in improved patient health and long-term recovery.

long_term_outlook.room_for_funding_explanation: The massive scale of medical needs in Syria and Gaza, combined with SAMS's rapid revenue growth, suggests they can effectively absorb more capital.

organizational_capacity.geographic_reach: Syria, Jordan, Lebanon, Turkey, Egypt, Iraq, Greece, Gaza, Libya, Ukraine

peer_comparison.differentiator: SAMS stands out with a <cite id="3">54.2% revenue growth rate</cite>, significantly outpacing many peers in the global health sector.

similar_organizations.differentiator: Similar healthcare focus and humanitarian relief size
similar_organizations.name: MedGlobal

similar_organizations.differentiator: Specialized focus on pediatric medical care in conflict zones
similar_organizations.name: Palestine Childrens Relief Fund

similar_organizations.differentiator: Global health focus with a larger emphasis on nutrition and vision
similar_organizations.name: Helen Keller International

strengths.detail: SAMS demonstrates remarkable fiscal discipline, spending only <cite id="9">$0.01 to raise every $1 in donations</cite>, which is significantly better than industry averages.
The foundation has documented specific clinical outcomes, including a <cite id="10">reduction in PTSD symptoms after therapy</cite> for conflict survivors.
The organization maintains a <cite id="11">100% independent board of directors</cite> and holds the highest transparency rating from Candid.

strengths_deep_dive: SAMS Foundation's operational model is built on direct medical intervention. Their financial health is robust, maintaining <cite id="12">4.6 months of working capital</cite> to ensure continuity of care in volatile regions. Their grantmaking capacity is also significant, distributing <cite id="13">$18.2 million in foreign grants</cite> to 184 recipients to support local medical infrastructure. This dual approach of direct service and local partnership allows them to reach <cite id="14">over 2,400 children in support groups</cite> across Jordan and Syria simultaneously.
rich_narrative.summary: The Syrian American Medical Society (SAMS) Foundation is a leading humanitarian organization that provides life-saving medical relief and specialized healthcare to vulnerable populations. <cite id="1">founded in 1998</cite>, the organization has grown into a critical medical provider, particularly in crisis zones. In 2023, SAMS reported <cite id="2">total revenue of $99,929,862</cite>, representing a significant <cite id="3">three-year revenue CAGR of 54.2%</cite>. The foundation's operations are characterized by a <cite id="4">program expense ratio of 95.6%</cite>, focusing heavily on direct service delivery. Beyond primary care, SAMS is a pioneer in addressing <cite id="5">Human Devastation Syndrome</cite>, providing specialized mental health and psychosocial support (MHPSS) to survivors of conflict. Their impact is substantial, having provided <cite id="6">3.5 million medical services</cite> in a single reporting period, with <cite id="7">3.2 million of those services delivered inside Syria</cite>. The organization maintains a <cite id="8">Platinum Seal of Transparency from Candid</cite>, reflecting its commitment to high-level data sharing and organizational accountability.
